titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Roxana Robinson Papers
titlestmt.author: by Emily Komornik

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.3830463

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 3, 2021 at 04:04

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Roxana Robinson papers consists of writings, correspondence and subject files, photographs, personal papers, teaching files, printed materials, computer media, and audiovisual media documenting the life and work of author Roxana Robinson, dated from circa 1960 to 2018.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 36 (financial and legal records): Restricted until 2046. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Box 42 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 43 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ken Lopez Bookseller on the Sinclair Lewis Fund, 2019.

bioghist.head: Roxana Robinson (1946-)
bioghist.p: Roxana Robinson is the author of over ten works of fiction and nonfiction, including the biography Georgia O'Keeffe: A Life (1989), widely considered one of the most comprehensive biographies on the artist. The Washington Post selected Robinson's novel Cost as one of the five best novels of 2008. Cost also received critical acclaim from the Chicago Tribune, Library Journal, The Seattle Times, and The Wall Street Journal, and was longlisted for the Dublin IMPAC Prize for Fiction. Additionally, her novel Sparta (2014) won the Maine Fiction award and was named one of the ten best books of the year by the BBC. Robinson has been awarded fellowships from the Guggenheim Foundation, the National Education Association, and the MacDowell Colony. She served as president of the Authors Guild from 2013 to 2017. Most recently, Robinson received the Barnes & Noble Writers for Writers Award in 2019.

Robinson was born on November 30, 1946 in Pine Mountain, Kentucky, and raised in New Hope, Pennsylvania. She is descended from several notable American families including the Beecher family (Henry Ward Beecher, Lyman Beecher, Harriet Beecher Stowe), the Trumbull family (Jonathan Trumbull), the Scoville family, and the Gallaudet family (Thomas Hopkins Gallaudet, pioneer of American education for the deaf).

Robinson received her B.A. in English Literature at the University of Michigan. Prior to writing fiction, she worked in the art department at Sotheby's and wrote about late nineteenth and early twentieth-century American art. She has taught at institutions including Wesleyan University, University of Houston, and The New School in New York City. She currently teaches in the MFA program at Hunter College.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Roxana Robinson Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing, indicated by brackets.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Roxana Robinson Papers consists of writings, correspondence, photographs, personal papers, teaching files, printed materials, computer media, and audiovisual media documenting the life and work of author Roxana Robinson, dated from circa 1960 to 2018. Writings include research files, notes, clippings, drafts, typescripts, correspondence, and interview transcripts related to the publication of Robinson's biography Georgia O'Keeffe: A Life and her play Georgia O'Keeffe, as well as six novels, and short stories featured in publications such as House and Garden, The New Yorker, The New York Times, and Vogue. Materials related to Robinson's research on O'Keeffe include copies of O'Keeffe's correspondence with Alfred Stieglitz and Anita Pollitzer, and Robinson's interview with Juan Hamilton. Correspondence contains over twenty years of correspondence between Robinson and Guislaine Morland, author of The Food of Love, as well as correspondence and subject files pertaining to Robinson's novels, short stories, and public appearances. Photographs consist of publicity stills of Robinson. Teaching files contain syllabi, course outlines, articles, and notes related to teaching positions Robinson held at institutions including the University of Houston, Wesleyan University, and The New School. Printed materials feature Robinson's novels, short stories, articles, essays, and reviews of her work by others. Computer and audiovisual media include interviews, drafts, and audio recordings on computer discs, floppy disks, optical media, videocassettes, and audiocassettes.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Roxana Robinson Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized into seven series: I. Writings, circa 1960-2018 and undated. II. Correspondence, 1978-2010 and undated. III. Photographs, circa 1985-2009 and undated. IV. Teaching Files, 1991-2008. V. Personal Papers, 1965-2009. VI. Printed Materials, 1974-2011. VII. Computer and Audiovisual Media, 1982-2005 and undated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains research files, notes, drafts, typescripts, page proofs, galley proofs, and correspondence related to Robinson's writings. A large portion of the series consists of research for Robinson's biography Georgia O'Keeffe: A Life, including materials gathered from the Alfred Stieglitz/Georgia O'Keeffe Archive at the Beinecke Library. There are also drafts, correspondence, and publicity for Robinson's play about O'Keeffe, and files for her short stories, articles, essays, reviews, and six novels.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Georgia O'Keeffe: A Life, Novels, and Shorter Works.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains Robinson's files for her biography Georgia O'Keeffe: A Life, including transcribed letters, photocopies of correspondence, typescripts, and interview transcripts, as well as Robinson's notes, notebooks, and drafts. Photocopies of materials from the Alfred Stieglitz/Georgia O'Keeffe Archive date from circa 1967, when Robinson conducted her research, while original materials Robinson consulted within the archive date from 1873.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in two groupings: Short Stories, and Articles and Essays.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Files within this grouping were originally housed in two accordion files bearing handwritten titles and notes by Robinson. During processing, the materials were removed and rehoused in folders, and the accordion file covers were retained.

did.unittitle: "What is it Exactly that You Do"

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three groupings: General, Travel, and Reviews. Arranged chronologically therein.

did.unittitle: The Parent Trap New York Times op ed
[ New York Times travel articles by Robinson]
Wharton New York Times book review

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence and subject files related to the publication of Robinson's novels, short stories, articles, and reviews, including correspondence with publishers, editors, and publicists regarding contracts, appearances, and press releases. It also includes over twenty years of correspondence with Guislaine Morland, author of The Food of Love.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Correspondence and Subject Files, and Correspondence with Guislaine Morland.

did.unittitle: Appearances, AWP [Association of Writers and Writing Programs]
The Atlantic [contracts and galleys, rejection letters, correspondence]
Farrar, Straus, and Giroux Publishers, Inc. authors' questionnaire
New York Times [contracts and publication correspondence]
This Is My Daughter , Random House
WGF [Willow Green Farm sketches and appraisal]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes professional photographs taken of Robinson for various publications, including Westchester WAG and The New York Times, and for book publicity. Also present are professional photographs taken of Robinson's niece, Wendy Richardson.

did.unittitle: Vogue photographs [Robinson and her daughter, Roxana]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains lecture notes, course outlines, syllabi, annotated articles, student course evaluations, notes, and research related to Robinson's teaching appointments at institutions such as Wesleyan University, The New School, and the University of Houston. Teaching files do not contain graded student coursework.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: General, University of Houston, Wesleyan University, and The New School. Arranged chronologically by teaching appointment.

did.unittitle: [Notebook pertaining to Robinson as visiting writer]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains legal records, including trusts, wills, and personal financial statements; calendar books, date books, and journals; and Robinson's student papers, such as notes, notebooks, and annotated articles from her time at Bennington College and Hunter College.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: General, Calendar Books, Diaries, and Journals, and Personal Financial. Arranged chronologically.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 36 (legal and financial records): Restricted until 2046. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: [Robinson's student papers, Bennington College and Hunter College]

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 36 (legal and financial records): Restricted until 2046. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials that either pertain to or feature Robinson's writing, such as articles, short stories, reviews by Robinson, reviews of Robinson's work, and interviews in publications including The Atlantic, House and Garden, The New Yorker, Vogue, and Women's Studies.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Publications and Clippings, and Newspapers. Arranged chronologically.

title.part: Shapes of Industry: First Images in American Art
Land of Plenty: The Katonah Gallery

did.unittitle: The New Fiction Magazine , volume 6, number 7
A Glimpse of Scarlet book jackets
Harper and Row A Glimpse of Scarlet reviews, United States

title.part: The New York Times Home Design

did.unittitle: The 22nd Annual NEBA Meeting and Trade Show
Asking for Love public relations materials
Asking for Love United Kingdom reviews
A Glimpse of Scarlet reviews and publicity
Q + A [ Chicago Tribune question and answer article]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of computer discs, floppy disks, videocassettes, and audiocassettes containing recordings of Robinson's lectures, interviews, and radio broadcasts, as well as revisions for some of Robinson's writings.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 42 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 43 (audiovisual media): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Computer Media, and Audiovisual Media. Arranged chronologically.
